title: Introduction to org
text: concept behind org fairly simple - analyses three main sections: Code Results Data sections unique requirements. Code : version controlled. publically accessible. single analysis pipeline detailing steps data cleaning, analysis, result generation. Results : immediately shared close collaborators. Save set results make accessible track changes time. Data : encrypted (sensitive). stored cloud (sensitive).

title: Introduction to org
text: org::initialize_project takes 2+ arguments. saves results (.e., folder locations) org::project, use subsequent code. home: Location Run.R R/ folder. Accessible via org::project$home (rarely need ). results: Results folder. Creates folder inside results todayŌĆÖs date, accessible via org::project$results_today (store results). folderŌĆÖs name todayŌĆÖs date, means automatically archives one copy results per day. allows collaborators easily check see results changed time. ...: Specify folders needed, also accessible via org::project (e.g., data_raw raw data, data_clean clean data).

title: Introduction to org
text: Run.R main file, sequentially running every part analysis: Data cleaning Analysis Result generation sections code encapsulated functions living R/ folder. main files, creates confusion need return code two years later. However, can different versions Run.R (e.g., Run_v01.R, Run_v02.R), implicitly understood later versions supersede earlier ones. Run_v03.R exists, Run_v02.R considered deprecated retained historical archival purposes, active use. sections code (e.g.┬Ādata cleaning, analyses table 1, analyses table 2, result generation table 1, sensitivity analysis 1, etc) encapsulated functions live R folder.

title: Introduction to org
code: # Define the following 'special' locations: # 'home' (i.e. Run.R and R/ folder) # 'results' (i.e. A folder with today's date will be created here)  # Define any other locations you want: # 'raw' (i.e. raw data)  org::initialize_project(   env = .GlobalEnv,   home = "/git/analyses/2019/analysis3/",   results = "/dropbox/analyses_results/2019/analysis3/"   data_raw = "/data/analyses/2019/analysis3/" )  # Describe changes in archived results day-to-day # Save this information in a text file in the `org::project$results` folder txt <- glue::glue("   2019-01-01:     Included:     - Table 1     - Table 2      2019-02-02:     Changed Table 1 from mean -> median    ", .trim=FALSE)  org::write_text(   txt = txt,   file = fs::path(org::project$results, "info.txt") )  library(data.table) library(ggplot2)  # This function accesses data located in org::project$data_raw d <- clean_data()  # These functions save results to org::project$results_today table_1(d) figure_1(d) figure_2(d)

title: Introduction to org
text: functions defined org::project$home/R. function org::initialize_project automatically sources R scripts org::project$home/R.

title: Introduction to org
text: writing research article, multiple versions often required (initial submission, followed multiple resubmission variants based recommendations peer reviewers). Rename Run.R R/ include submission date version preserve code produced results every submission. initial submission article, rename Run.R R/ Run_YYYY_MM_DD_submission_1.R R_YYYY_MM_DD_submission_1/ (YYYY_MM_DD replaced year, month, date). making adjustments resubmission, rename relevant files/folders run_YYYY_MM_DD_submission_2.R R_YYYY_MM_DD_submission_2/ (YYYY_MM_DD replaced year, month, date). , successfully preserve code produced results every submission, allowing easily ensure changes deliberate intentional.

title: Introduction to org

title: Introduction to org
text: single folder shared network drive, without access GitHub Dropbox, can create RMarkdown document following structure: Suggested code Run.R:
code: # code goes here: project_name <- org::project$home   +-- Run.R   +-- R   |      +-- CleanData.R   |      +-- Descriptives.R   |      +-- Analysis1.R   |      +-- Graphs1.R   +-- paper   |      +-- paper.Rmd   +-- results <- org::project$results   |         +-- 2018-03-12 <- org::project$results_today   |         |            +-- table1.xlsx   |         |            +-- figure1.png   |         +-- 2018-03-12 <- org::project$results_today   |         |            +-- table1.xlsx   |         |            +-- figure1.png   +-- data_raw <- org::project$raw   |          +-- data.xlsx # Initialize the project  org::initialize_project(   env = .GlobalEnv,   home = "/network/project_name/",   results = "/network/project_name/results/",   paper = "/network/paper/",   raw = "/network/project_name/data_raw/" )  # do some analyses here  # render the paper  rmarkdown::render(   input = fs::path(org::project$paper,"paper.Rmd"),   output_dir = org::project$results_today,   quiet = F )

what: One Folder on a Shared Network Drive Without GitHub Access
title: Introduction to org
text: one folder shared network drive, without GitHub Dropbox access, suggest following structure: Suggested code Run.R:
code: # code goes here: project_name <- org::project$home   +-- Run.R   +-- R   |      +-- clean_data.R   |      +-- descriptives.R   |      +-- analysis.R   |      +-- figure_1.R   +-- results <- org::project$results   |         +-- 2018-03-12 <- org::project$results_today   |         |            +-- table_1.xlsx   |         |            +-- figure_1.png   |         +-- 2018-03-12 <- org::project$results_today   |         |            +-- table_1.xlsx   |         |            +-- figure_1.png   +-- data_raw <- org::project$data_raw   |          +-- data.xlsx org::initialize_project(   env = .GlobalEnv,   home = "/network/project_name/",   results = "/network/project_name/results/",   data_raw = "/network/project_name/data_raw/" )

title: Introduction to org
text: Let us consider file /home/richard/test.src path location directory structure. Filenames include information name file. Directories include information name directory. Filepaths paths specify location file.

title: Organising Projects
text: org framework help organize projects. analyses three () main sections: code, results, data, different requirements (version control/sharing/encryption). provide folder locations ŌĆśorgŌĆÖ helps take care details. Read introduction vignette run help(package="org").

title: Organising Projects
text: csverse set R packages developed help solve problems frequently occur performing disease surveillance. want install dev versions (access packages havenŌĆÖt released CRAN), run usethis::edit_r_profile() edit .Rprofile. write : Save file restart R. can now install csverse packages drat repository.
code: options(   repos = structure(c(     CSVERSE = "https://www.csids.no/drat/",     CRAN    = "https://cran.rstudio.com"   )) ) install.packages("org")

title: Initialize project ŌĆö initialize_project
text: function initializes project setting folder locations sourcing code files. saves folder locations new environment org::project, use subsequent code. additional folder created user's file system (org::project$results_today) corresponds results/YYYY-MM-DD. sourced folders saved org::project$env.

title: Initialize project ŌĆö initialize_project
code: initialize_project(   env = new.env(),   home = NULL,   results = NULL,   folders_to_be_sourced = "R",   source_folders_absolute = FALSE,   encode_from = "UTF-8",   encode_to = "latin1",   ... )

title: Initialize project ŌĆö initialize_project
text: env environment code sourced (use .GlobalEnv source code global environment) home folder containing 'Run.R' 'R/' results folder inside results today's date created accessible via org::project$results_today (store results) folders_to_be_sourced names folders live inside home .r .R files inside sourced global environment. source_folders_absolute TRUE folders_to_be_sourced absolute folder reference. FALSE folders_to_be_sourced relative inside home. encode_from Folders current encoding (used Windows) encode_to Folders final encoding (used Windows) ... folders like reference

title: Initialize project ŌĆö initialize_project
text: Returns environment contains: Folder locations environment called env code sourced . also side effect org::project mirrors values.

what: Write text to a file ŌĆö write_text
title: Write text to a file ŌĆö write_text
text: function writes text file, optionally including header top file. text header converted Linux newline format Windows newline format writing.

title: Write text to a file ŌĆö write_text
code: write_text(   txt,   file = "",   header = "**THIS FILE IS CONSTANTLY OVERWRITTEN -- DO NOT MANUALLY EDIT**\r\n\r\n" )

title: Write text to a file ŌĆö write_text
text: txt character string text written file. file character string specifying file path. Passed base::cat. Default empty string, writes console. header optional character string header inserted top text file. Default **FILE CONSTANTLY OVERWRITTEN -- MANUALLY EDIT**\r\n\r\n.

title: Write text to a file ŌĆö write_text
text: return value. function called side effect writing file.

title: Write text to a file ŌĆö write_text
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ org::write_text("Sample text", "output.txt") org::write_text("Another piece of text", "output.txt", "Custom Header\r\n\r\n") } # }

title: Version 2025.3.10
text: package_installed can now install packages missing.

title: Version 2024.6.5
text: CRAN release: 2024-06-04 Fixed error org::initialize_project ŌĆ£//ŌĆØ start path removed.

title: Version 2022.12.28
text: Inclusion create_project_quarto provides example project use org quarto. Inclusion file utility functions, ls_files, move_file_or_dir, path. Inclusion utility function package_installed.

title: Version 2022.7.21
text: CRAN release: 2022-07-20 Reduction exports : initialize_project, set_results, write_text. initialize_project now takes env argument (environment functions sourced).

title: Version 2020.2.17
text: Introduction : - write_text - initialize_project - set_results - org::project Depreciation : - AllowFileManipulationFromInitialiseProject - InitialiseProject - PROJ - set_shared

title: Version 2019.4.2
text: CRAN release: 2019-04-02 Allows multiple code folders sourced using argument folders_to_be_sourced (previously hardcoded folder called code)

title: Version 2019.3.5
text: Removal stringr lubridate dependencies

title: Version 2019.2.21
text: CRAN release: 2019-03-06 Submission CRAN Includes functions AllowFileManipulationFromInitialiseProject InitialiseProject
